items.headline: Mavericks Match Knicks' Offer Sheet for Moussa Cisse
items.summary: The Dallas Mavericks swiftly matched the New York Knicks' two-year, $4.7 million offer sheet for center Moussa Cisse, ensuring the 23-year-old big man remains in Dallas. Cisse, who averaged 4.5 points and 5.7 rebounds last season, is seen as a key piece alongside Dereck Lively II and Daniel Gafford.

items.headline: LeBron James Narrows Free Agency Choices; NBA Awaits Decision
items.summary: LeBron James has reportedly focused his 2026 free agency decision to the Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat, and Philadelphia 76ers, with the Golden State Warriors also considered a 'longer-shot' option. The New York Knicks are reportedly out of the running. NBA Commissioner Adam Silver has urged James to make a decision soon to allow the league to finalize its schedule.

items.headline: Warriors Among Teams Eyeing Klay Thompson Trade
items.summary: Klay Thompson's expiring contract with the Dallas Mavericks has ignited trade speculation, with the Golden State Warriors reportedly leading the chase for a reunion, especially if they can secure LeBron James. The Miami Heat, Los Angeles Lakers, Detroit Pistons, and Orlando Magic are also noted as potential suitors for the sharpshooter.

items.headline: MEM Dominates HOU in Semifinal
items.summary: The Memphis Grizzlies built an early lead and never looked back, securing a 101-90 victory over the Houston Rockets in an NBA Summer League semifinal.

items.headline: GSW Edges LAL in Thriller
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 92-88 in a hard-fought Summer League semifinal.

items.headline: ORL Outlasts BOS
items.summary: The Orlando Magic fended off the Boston Celtics with a 103-97 victory in a consolation matchup.

items.headline: MIL Claims Close Win Over PHI
items.summary: The Milwaukee Bucks secured a narrow 96-94 win over the Philadelphia 76ers, highlighted by a game-winning layup.

items.headline: WAS Defeats ATL
items.summary: The Washington Wizards defeated the Atlanta Hawks 91-83 in their Summer League finale.

items.headline: Coward Leads Grizzlies in Semifinal Win
Burries Hits Game-Winner for Bucks
items.statContext: NBA Summer League vs PHI, including game-winning layup

items.headline: Camden Shines for Wizards
items.statContext: NBA Summer League Finale vs ATL

items.headline: Thiero's Impressive Double-Double
items.statLabel: 18 Pts (6-6 FG), 10 Reb, 3 Ast, 3 Stl, 1 Blk

items.headline: Reneau Dominant Off the Bench for Magic
NBA Probing Bucks Over Gary Trent Jr. Extension
items.summary: The NBA has launched an investigation into the Milwaukee Bucks regarding Gary Trent Jr.'s recent extension, examining whether the franchise breached collective bargaining rules through a 'prior agreement' before he officially became a free agent.

items.headline: Karl-Anthony Towns Crowns Victor Wembanyama 'Next NBA Superstar'
items.summary: During Fanatics Fest NYC, New York Knicks star Karl-Anthony Towns publicly declared Victor Wembanyama as the 'next NBA superstar,' praising the young Spurs phenom's generational talent.

items.headline: Jonathan Kuminga's Restricted Free Agency Remains Unresolved
items.summary: The future of Golden State Warriors forward Jonathan Kuminga, a restricted free agent, continues to be a major storyline. Sign-and-trade discussions are reportedly still a possibility if the Warriors cannot reach a new contract agreement.

items.headline: Injury Updates: Kessler, Gueye, Aldama Out Long-Term
items.summary: Utah Jazz center Walker Kessler is out for the 2025-26 season after undergoing left shoulder surgery for a torn labrum. Atlanta Hawks' Mouhamadou Gueye is projected to miss 3-4 months post-foot surgery, and Memphis Grizzlies' Santi Aldama had season-ending knee surgery in March.

items.headline: Summer League Championship Set: Grizzlies vs. Warriors Tonight
items.summary: The NBA Summer League culminates tonight with the Memphis Grizzlies facing off against the Golden State Warriors for the championship title at 6 p.m. ET. Both teams advanced after winning their respective semifinals yesterday.
